_example_mission: This is a fully worked difficulty 2 salvage mission demonstrating best practices
_design_notes: Player scavenges an abandoned trading post. Multiple approaches, skill checks, and risk/reward decisions. Estimated playtime: 8-12 minutes.
title: Ghosts of Commerce
description: The old trading post closed overnight when the exodus began. Traders fled, leaving inventory and equipment behind. Your sensors detect power cells and thruster components in the storage yard. But you're not the only scavenger who knows about this place.
objectives: Retrieve ship parts from the trading post

stages._design: Stage 1 establishes situation and offers three approach styles: direct, cautious, or skilled
stages.title: The Trading Post
stages.description: The trading post sits on the edge of town, half-buried in windblown sand. Solar panels still work, providing dim light to the interior. Two vehicles are parked outside—other scavengers got here first. You count three people moving inside, checking shelves and crates. Your scanner shows valuable parts in the storage yard out back.

choices._design_choice: Direct approach: Highest risk, fastest route, no bonuses
choices.text: Walk in the front door—assert your claim

success.text: You push through the door. Three scavengers turn to look at you—a grizzled older man, a woman with a shotgun, and a nervous kid. The man speaks: 'Post is claimed. Move along.' His hand rests near a weapon. The woman racks her shotgun for emphasis. This could get ugly.

choices._design_choice: Cautious approach: Lower risk, slower, small discovery bonus
choices.text: Circle around to the storage yard—avoid the scavengers

success.text: You slip around the building, staying low. The storage yard is cluttered but accessible. You spot the parts immediately: power cells in a locked cabinet, thruster components on a rack. Better yet, the scavengers inside haven't checked back here yet. You've got time to work.

choices._design_choice: Skilled approach: Engineering check, best outcome if successful, risky if failed
choices.text: Hack into the trading post's security system

success.text: You find a junction box and tap into the post's network. The system is old but functional. You access camera feeds showing the interior layout, scavenger positions, and—jackpot—the location of the trader's personal safe. With this intel, you can move efficiently.

failure.text: The security system locks you out and triggers an alarm. The scavengers inside hear it and rush toward the sound. You've lost the element of surprise.

stages._design: Confrontation path: Player chose aggressive entry or failed hacking. Diplomacy or negotiation required.
stages.title: Standoff
stages.description: The tension is thick. The three scavengers size you up. You're outnumbered, but they don't seem eager for a fight. The older man speaks again: 'Look, kid. We got here first. Fair's fair. Walk away, nobody gets hurt.'

conditional_text.if_aggressive_entry: Your aggressive entrance put them on edge. They're defensive.
conditional_text.if_triggered_alarm: The alarm spooked them. They think you're trouble.
conditional_text.default: They're cautious, but not hostile yet.

choices._design_choice: Diplomatic resolution: Skill check, peaceful outcome, moderate bonus
choices.text: Negotiate to split the salvage fairly

success.text: 'Fair point,' the old man says. 'You got guts coming in here. Alright—we take the interior, you take the storage yard. Even split. Deal?' You shake hands. The woman lowers her shotgun. The kid looks relieved. Crisis averted.

failure.text: The old man shakes his head. 'Nice try, but no deal. We were here first. Back off.' The woman chambers a round. You've got a choice: fight or retreat.

choices._design_choice: Retreat: Safe, no rewards, can return later via different path
choices.text: Back down—this isn't worth dying over

success.text: You raise your hands and back toward the door. 'Your post. I'll find parts elsewhere.' The tension breaks. You leave alive, but empty-handed. Maybe you can sneak back later through the storage yard.

choices._design_choice: Alternative path: Offer information trade instead of violence
choices.text: Offer to trade location intel for access to the yard

success.text: The old man's interest piques. 'Intel, huh? What you got?' You tell him about a depot three clicks south—military hardware, untouched. His eyes light up. 'Deal. Yard's yours. We'll check that depot.' They gather their gear and leave. You have the post to yourself.

failure.text: 'We already know about that depot,' the woman says. 'Try again.' Your bluff failed. They're not impressed.

stages._design: Uncontested storage yard: Player has access without conflict. Can choose thorough vs quick salvage.
stages.title: The Storage Yard
stages.description: The yard is yours. Crates and equipment racks stand in neat rows, covered in dust but intact. Your scanner highlights two priority items: a locked cabinet containing power cells, and a rack of thruster components. You also notice a floor hatch—could be a basement or cache.

conditional_text.if_has_full_intel: Your security hack shows the hatch leads to the trader's personal safe. Jackpot.
conditional_text.if_quiet_approach: You've got time. No one knows you're here.
conditional_text.default: Work fast. Someone could show up.

choices._design_choice: Quick salvage: Take visible items, leave fast, guaranteed success, lower rewards
choices.text: Take the thruster components and leave—don't push your luck

success.text: You grab the thruster components from the rack. Good quality, common rarity. Not a huge haul, but clean and fast. You're out before anyone notices. Success.

choices._design_choice: Force the cabinet: Risk/reward, chance of better parts or triggering alarm
choices.text: Force open the locked cabinet—risk an alarm for the power cells

success.text: The lock snaps with a satisfying crack. Inside: three high-quality power cells, uncommon rarity. Worth the risk. You load them into your bag along with the thrusters. Excellent haul.

failure.text: The cabinet alarm screams. You freeze. Footsteps—the scavengers from inside are coming. You've got seconds to decide: grab what you can and run, or try to silence the alarm.

choices._design_choice: Bypass lock: Engineering check, clean success, no risk
choices.text: Bypass the cabinet lock with engineering tools

success.text: You carefully manipulate the lock mechanism. Click. The cabinet opens silently. Power cells secured, no alarm. Professional work. You take the thrusters too. Perfect operation.

failure.text: The lock jams under your tools. You can't open it without forcing. The thrusters will have to be enough.

choices._design_choice: Investigate hatch: Optional discovery, potentially rare find
choices.text: Investigate the floor hatch before taking parts

success.text: You pull open the hatch. A ladder leads down to a small underground cache—the trader's personal stash. Unopened crates labeled 'Premium Stock.' This could be valuable.

stages._design: Personal cache: Optional discovery content, high skill check for best reward
stages.title: The Trader's Secret
stages.description: The underground cache is small but well-stocked. Premium parts the trader was saving for special customers. A locked strongbox sits in the corner—biometric seal still active. The parts on the shelves are yours for the taking.

choices._design_choice: Take shelf parts: Moderate reward, safe choice
choices.text: Take the parts from the shelves and leave the strongbox

success.text: You gather thruster components and a power cell from the shelves—uncommon quality. The strongbox remains sealed. You head back up with your haul. Solid find.

choices._design_choice: Force strongbox: High risk, potential rare parts or nothing
choices.text: Force open the strongbox—risk damage to contents

success.text: The strongbox cracks open. Inside: a rare propulsion system component, still in original packaging. The trader was hoarding the good stuff. This alone was worth the trip.

failure.text: The strongbox's internal failsafe activates—a small incendiary charge destroys the contents. Smoke and ash. You just destroyed whatever was inside. Take the shelf parts and go.

choices._design_choice: Science check: Analyze biometrics, safe opening, guaranteed rare part
choices.text: Analyze the biometric seal and trick it into opening

success.text: You study the biometric reader, then use the trader's personal datapad (found on the shelf) to synthesize an authorized fingerprint. The strongbox opens smoothly. Inside: a rare hybrid propulsion system. Perfect condition. You also take the shelf parts. Complete success.

failure.text: The biometric reader rejects your attempts and locks permanently. The shelf parts will have to suffice.

stages._design: Clean exit: Success path, mission complete, rewards calculated
stages.title: Successful Salvage
stages.description: You load the parts into your vehicle and prepare to leave. The trading post fades into the desert behind you. Another successful run. These parts will keep your ship flying.

conditional_text.if_perfect_discovery: You pulled off the perfect salvage. Every part secured, no alarms, no conflict. Professional.
conditional_text.if_got_rare_part: The rare component alone was worth the risk. This'll sell for a fortune, or upgrade your ship significantly.
conditional_text.if_negotiated_peace: You handled the scavengers diplomatically. They may remember you as someone who plays fair.
conditional_text.default: Parts secured. Job done. Time to head home.

success.text: The workshop never looked so good. You unload your haul and start cataloging the parts. Today was a good day.

stages.title: Mission Complete
stages.description: Salvage operation successful. Parts acquired. Credits earned.

_design_summary.total_possible_xp: Base 150 + Max bonuses 165 = 315 XP (excellent for difficulty 2)

possible_rewards.maximum: 1 common thruster + 1 common power cell + 1 rare propulsion + bonus credits

design_strengths: Skill checks reward specialization but aren't mandatory
Moral choices (negotiate vs retreat vs fight)
Varied endings based on player choices
Rewards scale with player skill and risk-taking
_design_summary.why_this_works: Players have agency, choices matter, multiple paths to success, skilled play rewarded but not required, replayable with different approaches
